label: Mild allergic rhinitis without wheeze or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit allergic rhinitis, also called hay fever or seasonal allergies.
found: Your clinician diagnosed mild allergic rhinitis. We did not find anaphylaxis, wheezing/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis, or another emergency problem today.
home: Avoid known allergy triggers when possible.
Wash your hands and face after heavy pollen or dust exposure.
Use saline spray or rinses only if your clinician says they are safe for you.
meds: Use allergy medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not mix sedating medicines, alcohol, or driving unless you know how the medicine affects you.
return: Trouble breathing, wheezing, throat/tongue swelling, fainting, or a bodywide allergic reaction.
Fever, severe facial pain, worsening one-sided sinus symptoms, or symptoms lasting longer than expected.
Symptoms do not improve with the plan you were given.
follow: Follow up with primary care or allergy care if symptoms are not improving, recur often, or interfere with sleep or breathing.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild allergic rhinitis.
No anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis concern, systemic illness, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, throat/tongue swelling, wheeze, asthma/COPD exacerbation, respiratory distress, or hypoxia.
Bacterial sinusitis concern, severe facial pain, fever, orbital symptoms, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports allergic-rhinitis/hay-fever framing, common nasal/eye symptoms, trigger reduction, clinician-recommended medicines, and escalation for severe allergy symptoms, non-improvement, or worsening wheeze/cough. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild allergic rhinitis without anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze/asthma flare, bacterial sinusitis complication, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild allergic rhinitis
no anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, wheeze, asthma flare, or trouble breathing documented
no bacterial sinusitis complication, systemic illness, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed allergy pathway

label: Localized plantar wart without diabetic foot or immunocompromised host
summary: Your symptoms fit a plantar wart, a wart on the bottom of the foot.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a localized plantar wart. We did not find a diabetic-foot wound, skin infection, genital wart pathway, or immunocompromised-host risk today.
home: Keep the area clean and dry.
Do not pick, cut, or dig into the wart.
Avoid sharing towels, razors, nail tools, or footwear.
meds: Use wart medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use acid or freezing treatments on broken skin or high-risk feet unless your clinician told you to.
return: Spreading redness, pus, fever, red streaks, or severe worsening pain.
New foot wound, numbness, black skin, or trouble walking.
Warts are genital, rapidly spreading, bleeding, or occurring with immune compromise.
follow: Follow up with primary care, dermatology, or podiatry if pain persists, walking is affected, or treatment is not working.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized plantar wart.
No diabetic-foot pathway, open wound, infection, immunocompromised host, genital wart, severe pain/ambulation problem, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Diabetes with foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, open wound, cellulitis, abscess, bleeding wart, severe pain, inability to walk, or immunocompromised host.
Genital wart/STI pathway, mucosal wart, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry/dermatology plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports wart/plantar-wart framing, HPV cause, foot location, spread precautions, and separating genital warts from common/plantar wart pathways. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized plantar wart without diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, open wound, infection, immunocompromised host, genital/mucosal pathway, severe ambulation problem, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized plantar wart
no diabetic foot, foot wound, infection, or immunocompromised host documented
no genital/mucosal, severe ambulation, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway

label: Uncomplicated corn or callus without diabetic foot or infection
summary: Your symptoms fit a corn or callus, thickened skin from pressure or friction.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an uncomplicated corn or callus. We did not find infection, an open wound, or diabetic-foot risk requiring a different plan today.
home: Reduce pressure or friction on the area when possible.
Wear comfortable footwear or protective padding if recommended.
Do not cut or shave thickened skin yourself.
meds: Use corn/callus products only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use acid pads or sharp tools on the feet if you have diabetes, numbness, poor circulation, or broken skin.
return: Spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, red streaks, or worsening pain.
Open sore, bleeding crack, black skin, numbness, or new foot wound.
Pain prevents walking or the area is not improving with pressure reduction.
follow: Follow up with primary care or podiatry if pain persists, walking is affected, or you have diabetes, poor circulation, or numbness.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated corn or callus.
No open wound, secondary infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, severe pain, inability to walk, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Diabetic foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, open wound, ulcer, cellulitis, abscess, bleeding crack, black skin, or systemic illness.
Severe pain, inability to walk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports corn/callus framing as thickened skin from pressure or friction, with pressure reduction and protective measures. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated corn/callus without open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, severe ambulation problem, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated corn or callus
no diabetic foot, open wound, foot wound, cellulitis, or secondary infection documented
no PVD/neuropathy, severe ambulation, systemic illness, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway

label: Mild acne without abscess or medication red flags
found: Your clinician diagnosed mild acne. We did not find abscess, cellulitis, severe cystic disease, medication emergency, or systemic illness today.
home: Wash skin gently and avoid scrubbing.
Do not pick, squeeze, or pop pimples.
Use non-comedogenic skin products when possible.
meds: Use acne medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Stop a product and seek care if you develop severe irritation, swelling, blistering, or peeling.
return: Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus pocket, severe pain, or rapidly worsening swelling.
Skin peeling, mouth sores, target-like rash, facial swelling, or trouble breathing after a medicine.
Severe cysts, scarring, pregnancy medication concerns, or symptoms not improving with the plan.
follow: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms are severe, scarring, worsening, or not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild acne.
No abscess, cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular disease, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, pregnancy medication issue, systemic illness, or specialist-directed medication plan.
exclusion: Abscess, cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular acne, rapidly worsening swelling, systemic illness, or severe cutaneous adverse reaction.
Pregnancy medication decision, isotretinoin/teratogenic medication issue, immunocompromised host, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports acne framing, gentle skin care, avoiding picking/squeezing, clinician-directed medication use, and escalation for severe/worsening, scarring, or medication-related concerns. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed mild acne without abscess/cellulitis, severe cystic/nodular disease, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, pregnancy medication issue, systemic illness, immunocompromised/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed medication plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of mild acne
no abscess, cellulitis, severe acne, medication reaction, or systemic illness documented
no pregnancy medication, immunocompromised, pediatric, or specialist-directed dermatology pathway

label: Dry skin or xerosis without infection or systemic symptoms
summary: Your symptoms fit dry skin, also called xerosis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated dry skin. We did not find infection, severe eczema, drug-rash warning signs, or systemic illness today.
home: Use fragrance-free moisturizer often, especially after washing.
Use warm, not hot, water and gentle cleansers.
Avoid scratching when possible and protect cracked skin from irritation.
meds: Use skin medicine only if prescribed or recommended.
Avoid harsh products, alcohol-based products, or steroid creams unless your clinician told you to use them.
return: Spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, red streaks, or worsening pain.
Bleeding cracks that do not heal, severe itching, widespread rash, or skin peeling/blistering.
Symptoms do not improve with the plan you were given.
follow: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if symptoms persist, recur, or become severe.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated dry skin or xerosis.
No secondary infection, severe eczema/psoriasis pathway, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, systemic illness, high-risk host, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Cellulitis, abscess, pus, bleeding fissures requiring wound care, systemic illness, severe itch with widespread rash, or severe cutaneous adverse reaction.
Severe eczema/psoriasis flare, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports dry-skin/xerosis framing, moisturizers, gentle skin care, avoiding drying triggers, and escalation for cracks that bleed, infection signs, severe itching, or non-improvement. Autonomous Phase 1701-1750 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated dry skin/xerosis without infection, severe eczema/psoriasis pathway, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, systemic illness, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated dry skin or xerosis
no cellulitis, secondary infection, fever, systemic symptoms, or systemic illness documented
no severe eczema/psoriasis, severe drug-rash, high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
